report_name: Iranian APT MuddyWater Masquerades as Ransomware Group in Microsoft Teams-Based Espionage Campaign

threat_actors.name: MuddyWater
threat_actors.description: An Iranian state-sponsored APT group identified as the actor behind a novel espionage campaign starting in early 2026. The group leverages Microsoft Teams for social engineering and masquerades as a ransomware attack to exfiltrate data and establish persistence on networks of strategic interest to Iran.
threat_actors.goals: Cyber espionage and long-term data exfiltration from organizations of strategic value to Iran.

threat_actors.name: Chaos ransomware group
threat_actors.description: A ransomware group identity that MuddyWater masqueraded as in a false flag operation to mislead incident responders and obscure the state-sponsored nature of their espionage campaign.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing via Service
attack_patterns.description: Attackers used Microsoft Teams as the delivery vehicle for a social engineering attack, persuading targets to join a meeting to harvest credentials.

attack_patterns.name: Credentials In Files
attack_patterns.description: During a Microsoft Teams screen-sharing session, attackers manipulated the victim into typing credentials into a text file, allowing for their capture.

attack_patterns.name: Remote Access Software
attack_patterns.description: The MuddyWater group deployed legitimate remote management and monitoring (RMM) tools, specifically DWAgent and AnyDesk, to maintain persistent access and evade detection.

attack_patterns.name: Masquerading
attack_patterns.description: The espionage operation mimicked the tactics of the Chaos ransomware group as a false flag to misdirect forensic analysis towards a criminal, rather than state-sponsored, origin.

attack_patterns.name: Web Protocols
attack_patterns.description: The deployed remote access trojans and RMM tools communicated with command and control infrastructure over standard web protocols to blend in with normal traffic.

attack_patterns.name: PowerShell
attack_patterns.description: Used to execute malicious scripts with a bypassed execution policy, as suggested by hunting hints for 'powershell.exe -ExecutionPolicy Bypass'.

malware.name: Game.exe
malware.description: A custom remote access trojan deployed by MuddyWater after gaining initial access.

malware.name: ms_upd.exe
malware.description: A malicious executable signed with a code-signing certificate issued to 'Donald Gay', which has been previously linked to MuddyWater operations.

malware.name: CastleRAT
malware.description: An off-the-shelf malware from the cybercrime ecosystem used by MuddyWater to obscure attribution.

malware.name: Tsundere
malware.description: An off-the-shelf malware from the cybercrime ecosystem used by MuddyWater to blur the lines between their operations and those of financially motivated criminals.

malware.name: Chaos
malware.description: A ransomware family whose group identity was impersonated by MuddyWater as part of a false flag operation.

tools.name: DWAgent
tools.description: A legitimate remote management and monitoring (RMM) tool deployed by MuddyWater to maintain a persistent foothold on compromised networks.

tools.name: AnyDesk
tools.description: A legitimate remote access software used by MuddyWater post-compromise for persistence and data exfiltration.

tools.name: Microsoft Teams
tools.description: Used as the platform for the social engineering attack, where attackers engaged victims in interactive screen-sharing sessions to harvest credentials.

campaigns.name: MuddyWater Teams Espionage Campaign 2026
campaigns.description: An espionage campaign beginning in early 2026 where MuddyWater used Microsoft Teams for social engineering and masqueraded as a ransomware group to exfiltrate data from strategic targets.

identities.name: Government Entities
identities.description: A class of organizations targeted by MuddyWater's 2026 espionage campaign.

identities.name: Organizations of strategic interest to Iran
identities.description: A broad category of organizations targeted by MuddyWater across the U.S., Europe, APAC, and the Middle East.

identities.name: RouterHosting
identities.description: A Virtual Private Server (VPS) provider whose infrastructure was used by MuddyWater in their attack campaign.

infrastructure.name: MuddyWater C2 Server
infrastructure.description: Command and Control server used in the 2026 espionage campaign, identified by IP 172.86.126.208.

infrastructure.name: MuddyWater Attack VPS
infrastructure.description: A RouterHosting VPS used as part of the attack infrastructure, identified by IP 172.86.76.127.

indicators.name: MuddyWater C2 Network IOCs 2026
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: MuddyWater C2 Network Connection 2026
id: 9c5b1d4a-8f3e-4b2c-9d6a-1e2f3a4b5c6d
status: experimental
description: Detects outbound network connections to known MuddyWater C2 infrastructure observed in a 2026 espionage campaign.
references:
  - https://www.thehackernews.com/2026/05/muddywater-uses-microsoft-teams-to.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-07
tags:
  - attack.t1071.001
  - attack.g0069
level: high
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    DestinationIp:
      - '172.86.126.208'
      - '172.86.76.127'
    Initiated: true
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects network traffic to command and control servers used by MuddyWater in their 2026 espionage campaign.

indicators.name: MuddyWater Malware Filename IOCs 2026
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: MuddyWater Malware Execution by Filename 2026
id: a1b2c3d4-e5f6-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of malicious files by name, as used by MuddyWater in a 2026 espionage campaign.
references:
  - https://www.thehackernews.com/2026/05/muddywater-uses-microsoft-teams-to.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-07
tags:
  - attack.g0069
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\ms_upd.exe'
      - '\Game.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects malicious executables used by MuddyWater based on their filenames.

indicators.name: Suspicious RMM Tool Execution (DWAgent, AnyDesk)
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious RMM Tool Execution
id: f8e7d6c5-b4a3-4f2e-1d0c-9b8a7f6e5d4c
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of DWAgent or AnyDesk, which were used by MuddyWater for persistence. This may generate false positives in environments where these tools are used legitimately.
references:
  - https://www.thehackernews.com/2026/05/muddywater-uses-microsoft-teams-to.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-07
tags:
  - attack.t1219
  - attack.g0069
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\DWAService.exe'
      - '\AnyDesk.exe'
  filter_legit_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\explorer.exe'
      - '\services.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit_parent
indicators.description: Detects the execution of DWAgent (DWAService.exe) or AnyDesk.exe, which could indicate abuse by MuddyWater for persistence.

indicators.name: Suspicious PowerShell Execution Policy Bypass
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious PowerShell Execution Policy Bypass
id: c9b8a7f6-e5d4-4c3b-2a1f-0e9d8c7b6a5b
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of 'powershell.exe -ExecutionPolicy Bypass', a technique often used to load malicious scripts, as noted in the MuddyWater campaign analysis.
references:
  - https://www.thehackernews.com/2026/05/muddywater-uses-microsoft-teams-to.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-07
tags:
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.g0069
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\powershell.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: ' -ExecutionPolicy Bypass'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects PowerShell being launched with the ExecutionPolicy Bypass flag, a common technique for running untrusted scripts.

relationships.target_name: Organizations of strategic interest to Iran
